title: cassia occidentalis var. entreriana
intro: Cassia occidentalis var. entreriana, also known as Senna, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and pod-like fruit. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a laxative due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The health benefits of Senna include its potential to reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improve digestion, and support weight loss. Senna can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, capsules, or tinctures, which should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
